title: Conflict resolution

publication.changeSummary: Topic-specific draft for Conflict resolution; requires human source, technical, pedagogy, and accessibility review. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: Git Reference Documentation; current stable accessed 2026-07; section applicable to Conflict resolution
sources.claim: A conflict records multiple unresolved versions in the index; resolution requires understanding both intents, editing a coherent result, removing markers, testing, and staging the resolved content.

sources.locator: Pro Git; second edition online; section applicable to Conflict resolution
sources.claim: A conflict records multiple unresolved versions in the index; resolution requires understanding both intents, editing a coherent result, removing markers, testing, and staging the resolved content.

canonicalExplanation.summary: A conflict records multiple unresolved versions in the index; resolution requires understanding both intents, editing a coherent result, removing markers, testing, and staging the resolved content.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: Two branches change the same validation branch differently, so the resolver reads both commits and writes one rule that preserves required cases from each. Your goal is to resolve a content conflict and document why the final behavior is correct. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Commits and history; Fast-forward and three-way merge; Diffs and patch review. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Operational model
sections.body: A conflict records multiple unresolved versions in the index; resolution requires understanding both intents, editing a coherent result, removing markers, testing, and staging the resolved content. The learner should use this model to predict an observable result rather than merely repeat terminology.

sections.heading: Boundary and failure point
sections.body: Choosing ours or theirs can discard valid work, conflicts can involve rename/delete and binary states, and a resolved text can still be semantically wrong. A correct explanation therefore includes the applicable assumptions and avoids this common mistake: Conflict resolution means deleting marker lines and choosing the version that compiles first.

includes: Interpret base, ours and theirs, edit the intended combined result and test before completing integration.
Resolve a content conflict and document why the final behavior is correct.
excludes: Choosing ours or theirs can discard valid work, conflicts can involve rename/delete and binary states, and a resolved text can still be semantically wrong.

outcomes: Resolve a content conflict and document why the final behavior is correct.

examples.title: Conflict resolution: direct case
examples.situation: Two branches change the same validation branch differently, so the resolver reads both commits and writes one rule that preserves required cases from each.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Observe the starting evidence: Two branches change the same validation branch differently, so the resolver reads both commits and writes one rule that preserves required cases from each.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Check the result against this correction: Recover both change intentions, construct and test the correct combined behavior, then stage only after no marker or unresolved index entry remains.
examples.result: The case supports the declared outcome because Recover both change intentions, construct and test the correct combined behavior, then stage only after no marker or unresolved index entry remains.

examples.title: Conflict resolution: boundary case
examples.situation: After removing conflict markers, status and staged diff are inspected and the focused test suite runs before the merge is completed.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Identify the changed context: After removing conflict markers, status and staged diff are inspected and the focused test suite runs before the merge is completed.
Step 2 — Keep this boundary explicit: Choosing ours or theirs can discard valid work, conflicts can involve rename/delete and binary states, and a resolved text can still be semantically wrong.
Step 3 — Reject the documented misconception: Conflict resolution means deleting marker lines and choosing the version that compiles first.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Resolve a content conflict and document why the final behavior is correct.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: Conflict resolution means deleting marker lines and choosing the version that compiles first.
misconceptions.correction: Recover both change intentions, construct and test the correct combined behavior, then stage only after no marker or unresolved index entry remains.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Using the direct and boundary scenarios, explain why this belief fails: Conflict resolution means deleting marker lines and choosing the version that compiles first.

analogy.example: A route map can model Conflict resolution: movable signposts identify current destinations, routes diverge, and integration reconnects selected paths.
usefulFor: Visualizing references, divergence, ancestry, merge bases, and reconciled paths
limitations: Branches are lightweight references to commits, not copied roads or independent containers of files.

recap.summary: You should now be able to resolve a content conflict and document why the final behavior is correct. Keep this boundary in view: Choosing ours or theirs can discard valid work, conflicts can involve rename/delete and binary states, and a resolved text can still be semantically wrong.
retrievalPrompts: Explain Conflict resolution without using the optional analogy and include one observable consequence.
Why is this statement incorrect: Conflict resolution means deleting marker lines and choosing the version that compiles first.
Apply the model to this case from memory: After removing conflict markers, status and staged diff are inspected and the focused test suite runs before the merge is completed.
recap.nextReviewPrompt: Next: continue to “Clone and remote configuration”. Before moving on, answer one retrieval prompt without reopening the explanation.
